rejection_reasons: The workflow is documentation-first and no medication plan was inferred.
The workflow is not limited to one joint or surgical population.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for middle-aged and older adults with typical OA-compatible symptoms, especially hip or knee; not exact for all joints, inflammatory arthritis, or acute injury.
setting_applicability: Partial: DHA-licensed telehealth versus in-person multi-joint follow-up.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; wider UAE use is not asserted.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2, effective 2024-04-21 and due for revision 2029-02-21, was reopened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: Issue 2 remains current on the DHA telehealth guidance portal.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped OA-compatible symptoms, function, quality-of-life, and assessed joint findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usage-related joint pain, stiffness, motion restriction, tenderness, swelling, instability, functional limitation, night pain, fever, redness or heat, weight loss, and systemic concern features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to confirm the guideline is strongest for knee and hip osteoarthritis; no multi-joint legacy items were mapped from that section alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes knee pain location, stairs-related pain, giving way, instability, and hip groin or thigh pain, stiffness, walking pain, restricted movement, and early loss of internal rotation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped OA terminology and clinician-entered X-ray or prior-imaging review without inferring diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinical diagnosis in typical adults, cautions that radiographic findings may not match symptoms, and limits MRI or other imaging to selected uncertainty or alternative-diagnosis contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped worsening, acute swelling, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines concern and specialist contexts including a hot swollen joint, persistent fever, night symptoms, weight loss, face-to-face assessment needs, worsening symptoms, severe functional limitation, and selected imaging.

unresolved_source_gaps: Analgesia use, lifestyle or exercise, weight management, physiotherapy, joint protection, walking aids, activity modification, and fixed three-to-six-month timing remain unsupported by the reviewed exact sections.
Osteoarthritis remains a clinician-stated diagnosis and radiographic findings must not be interpreted automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
